_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description:
You can:
- guide the organisation to ensure it has the specialist digital, data and technology skills it needs
- develop organisational processes and ways of working so that digital and data roles you support can thrive
- continuously improve and optimise the organisational environment
Examples of leadership using this skill:
- prioritising capability needs that will have the most impact
- negotiating for longer-term investment in people by articulating the risks and benefits of different staffing strategies
- advocating for good practice in ways of working and supporting people to adopt this practice
- growing digital and data communities
reference_document_element_description: Skill in designing data and AI training programs for diverse organizational audiences, from executives to operational staff, with role-appropriate content and delivery formats.
comments: DDaT skill superset of WIDAI OP domain. OP-S-002 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: DG-S-006 (DG, cosine: 0.558), DG-S-003 (DG, cosine: 0.557).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 5.2/10 (raw 0.521). Shared vocabulary: [data, skill]. DDaT-only terms [adopt, advocating, articulating, benefits, capability, communities] vs WIDAI-only terms [ai, appropriate, audiences, content, delivery, designing] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.839 (P=0.821, R=0.858). R exceeds P by 0.038 — the WIDAI KSA's 23 words are well-captured by the DDaT element's 100 words, but the DDaT element contains broader scope not fully reflected in the matched WIDAI KSA.

nli_cross_encoder.significance:
NLI: neutral-dominant (99.8%). "You can:
- guide the organisation to ensure it has the specialist digital, data" and "Skill in designing data and AI training programs for diverse organizational audi" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.547 ≈ STS 0.521 (gap 0.026). Independent encoding and cross-attention agree. Shared terms [data, skill] are sufficient for independent encoding to detect the match.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 7.5%. Shared tokens: [data, skill]. DDaT-only: [adopt, advocating, articulating, benefits, capability, communities]. WIDAI-only: [ai, appropriate, audiences, content, delivery, designing].

signals.detail: STS 0.521 barely clears classification threshold for Superset of. This match is fragile and may indicate insufficient KSA coverage for this competency area.
